exercise.details: Siberian Huskies were bred to run 100+ miles per day in sub-zero temperatures. They need at minimum 2 hours of vigorous exercise daily. Running, hiking, bikejoring, skijoring, and dog sledding are ideal outlets. They must be on a leash or in a securely fenced area at all times — Huskies are escape artists and will run for miles if loose. Do NOT exercise in heat above 60°F (15°C) intensely; they overheat quickly.

grooming.brushing_frequency: 2-3 times per week; daily during shedding
grooming.details: Huskies have a thick double coat that sheds moderately year-round and massively twice yearly ('blowing coat') for 3-5 weeks. During blowouts, daily brushing with an undercoat rake is essential or the coat becomes severely matted. Never shave a Husky — the double coat insulates against both heat and cold. Huskies are naturally clean dogs and rarely need bathing.

diet.notes: Huskies are uniquely efficient metabolically — they use calories more slowly than most breeds. Avoid overfeeding; they maintain weight on less food than expected. High-quality protein sources support their athletic needs. Huskies can be picky eaters and may skip meals — this is normal.

health.vet_checkups: annually; eye exams by veterinary ophthalmologist recommended
health.notes: Huskies can develop zinc deficiency even on adequate diets due to poor zinc absorption. Signs include crusty nose and paw pads, hair loss, and skin lesions — zinc supplementation resolves it. Eye conditions are common; annual eye exams are recommended.

temperament.trainability: moderate — intelligent but stubborn and independent
temperament.notes: Huskies are friendly, outgoing, and rarely aggressive, but they have a strong prey drive and should not be trusted off-leash. They are notorious escape artists — standard fencing is insufficient; they require 6-foot+ privacy fences and checked underground barriers. They howl rather than bark. They are pack animals and dislike being alone. Not recommended for first-time owners.

care_summary: Siberian Huskies are high-endurance working dogs requiring 2+ hours of vigorous daily exercise and extreme containment measures. Their seasonal shedding is significant and requires daily brushing during blowouts. They are friendly and pack-oriented but have a strong prey drive and will roam if given the chance. Experienced, active owners only.
